title: The Top Five Steven Soderbergh Films | Discussion (Ep. 125)
format: Sean, Amanda, and Chris review Soderbergh's Netflix release 'High Flying Bird,' then each rank their five favorite Soderbergh films.

films.title: High Flying Bird
films.note: Shot on an iPhone, written by Tarell Alvin McCraney: an NBA agent (Andre Holland) maneuvers his own client into a lockout standoff to force a new labor deal. Chris calls it his favorite Soderbergh since Haywire; all three note the parallel to Soderbergh's own fight with studios over marketing and distribution, most recently on Logan Lucky.

segments.heading: Amanda Dobbins's Top Five Soderbergh Movies
segments.subject: Steven Soderbergh

films.title: Ocean's Eleven
films.note: No. 1: the fizz and efficiency she values most in Soderbergh, a movie that never forgets to be fun to watch.

films.title: Out of Sight
films.note: No. 2: the Detroit hotel scene with the lighter is one of the best five scenes she's ever seen.

films.title: Magic Mike
films.note: No. 3: made for about $7 million on a deferred-fee bet with Channing Tatum, it made $170 million.

films.title: Ocean's Twelve
films.note: No. 4: her pick for the best sequel ever made, ahead of even Godfather Part II, which she rules a continuation rather than a sequel.

films.title: Erin Brockovich
films.note: No. 5: Soderbergh doing smart genre, a legal thriller that lets Julia Roberts play against her own persona.

segments.heading: Chris Ryan's Top Five Soderbergh Movies
segments.note: No. 1 wasn't revealed on air (a running joke about Kafka/Bubble stood in); No. 4 was a cheat pick, HBO's The Nick.
segments.subject: Steven Soderbergh

films.title: The Limey
films.note: No. 2: written by frequent collaborator Lem Dobbs, a 'man on a mission' movie in the same vein as High Flying Bird.

films.title: Haywire
films.note: No. 3: the Channing Tatum/Gina Carano diner fight is his favorite Soderbergh scene outside his top two.

films.title: Che: Part One
films.note: No. 5: a four-hour, chapter-by-chapter biopic Soderbergh has said he was relieved to finish.

segments.heading: Sean Fennessey's Top Five Soderbergh Movies
segments.subject: Steven Soderbergh

films.title: Out of Sight
films.note: No. 1: also Amanda's No. 2 pick, discussed together as the most romantic movie Soderbergh has made.

films.title: Sex, Lies, and Videotape
films.note: No. 3: his debut, which Sean says fits his theory that a great filmmaker's first film reveals everything, alongside Fincher's Alien 3 and Tarantino's Reservoir Dogs.

films.title: The Informant!
films.note: No. 4 (three-way tie): the Scott Z. Burns trilogy, Soderbergh paying homage to a genre he loves while making it his own.

films.title: Contagion
films.note: No. 4 (three-way tie): his homage to '70s outbreak-disaster movies, and home to Gwyneth Paltrow's death scene.

films.title: Side Effects
films.note: No. 4 (three-way tie): his take on the erotic thriller, with a script hinge Sean rates as underrated.

films.title: Ocean's Eleven
films.note: No. 5: also Amanda's No. 1 pick, saved for the same discussion later in the countdown.
